response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with a strong web and backend background, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his resume. His most hardware-adjacent work is a wave field synthesis bachelor thesis involving C++ and audio hardware, and some low-latency microservices work, but neither translates to the silicon-level performance engineering this role requires. His recent AI-adjacent work (MCP frameworks, agentic tooling) is purely software and does not address the deep-learning accelerator domain the team operates in.
strengths: Long career (~16 years) with consistent growth from developer to staff/senior IC roles, demonstrating sustained technical depth
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) shows some performance-oriented engineering mindset
Exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq indicates awareness of modern AI workflows
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role
No hardware architecture background (GPU, SIMD, MIMD, AI accelerators) anywhere in the resume
No deep learning training or inference optimization experience; AI involvement is limited to software-layer agentic tooling

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience
No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
Solid general software engineering tenure (16 years, staff-level roles)
